---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0doc-extract-memory-model
key: sv0doc-extract-memory-model
state: done
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0doc
    - memory
    - ownership
title: "extract memory model specification"
type: task
---

# extract memory model specification

## purpose

extract the memory model specification from the sv0 design document into a
formal specification at `sv0doc/memory-model/ownership.md`. the memory model
is central to sv0's safety guarantees and drives the borrow checker
implementation in sv0c.

## dependencies

/require: bash

## configuration

/env: SV0DOC_ROOT=sv0doc
/env: MEMORY_FILE=$SV0DOC_ROOT/memory-model/ownership.md

## extraction

/ai: extract the complete memory model specification from the sv0 design document.

     organize into these sections:

     1. ownership
        - every value has exactly one owner
        - assignment transfers ownership (move semantics)
        - previous binding invalidated after move
        - scope-based deallocation (RAII)

     2. Copy and Clone
        - Copy: bitwise copy, auto-derived when all fields are Copy
        - explicit impl Copy for documentation
        - types with Drop cannot be Copy
        - Clone: explicit duplication via .clone()
        - Copy implies Clone

     3. references and the exclusivity rule
        - &T: shared (read-only) reference
        - &mut T: exclusive (mutable) reference
        - at any point: either one &mut T OR any number of &T, never both
        - auto-dereferencing for method calls

     4. borrow tracking tiers
        - tier 1 (automatic): local borrow/move tracking within function bodies
        - tier 2 (elision): single-input or &self -> output borrows automatically
          - rule 1: one input ref -> output borrows from it
          - rule 2: &self -> output borrows from self
          - rule 3: otherwise, programmer must annotate
        - tier 3 (explicit): borrows(x, y) contract for multi-ref returns
          - replaces Rust lifetime parameters
          - borrows on struct declarations for reference-holding structs

     5. memory contracts
        - no_alias(a, b): non-aliasing assertion
        - old(expr) in ensures: pre-state comparison
        - borrows(x, y): borrow-source declaration

     6. allocation
        - stack allocation: default for fixed-size types
        - Box<T>: explicit heap allocation
        - Vec<T>, string: heap-backed collections
        - RAII: destructors run when owner goes out of scope

     7. unsafe
        - unsafe blocks opt out of borrow checking
        - contracts still enforced inside unsafe
        - required for: raw pointer dereference, FFI, mutable statics
        - raw pointers: *const T, *mut T
        - integer-to-pointer casts

     write the complete specification to sv0doc/memory-model/ownership.md.

/run: 01-validate-memory-model.sh

## validation

/assert: test -f $MEMORY_FILE
/assert: test -s $MEMORY_FILE
